layout: default
title: "Parliamentary Updates and Legislative Developments"
date: 2026-06-29T22:00:42.619705+00:00
summary: "Parliament passes the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, while legal challenges and political debates over quotas and institutional oversight continue"
run_time_ist: "3:30AM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Political Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 3:30AM</p>


## India National Politics

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Legislative milestone:</strong> The Rajya Sabha has passed the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, marking a significant development in national social legislation.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiuwFBVV95cUxOS1Y3RmZzYUVKcWZEU082Q3J2S210X3k5NEVLdmVkbTY2TlBsNFZIR2FoWFBrQTFaMDYwcXE3clIzTDN4VV83US1LUmxwd2FVeVZsNW5YeVZHU0ZMUnJLZjhTVjBnRFE2Y2ZpZTMtOWVqYU1TX3BNTHRvZjhscE1yT1JsRjVFYnFITm9GbVVkNEdkVjFrSTNvYldVUU1zSjlGaVZoZDE3T0x1Vmx5VW5ONnNvc0xxQU5kTlkw?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I1</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Judicial and political disputes:</strong> The Supreme Court has denied relief to Meenakshi Natarajan regarding her Rajya Sabha nomination, while Mallikarjun Kharge has been sworn in following his re-election.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxQcFlqUDlzckpoVE0xaFJfamMxclFVNXA1QWpTWThPZ3h2dWhtUmVSSTh3akc4TF9VMXJYcmZYQnpLZnU4V3JwV2UtVTdKdkw0VEoyb2preE9FOHlCZ0QyNVpDcnN0OGVVdUZKLU5FM1ROUDdKeFAtLVlyX2tISGVHRUkxbUdfT2YyZzkxMWd5a3BlMVh4M0JCanpiSG1TbGV5dE13bXRjTFNlQQ?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I2</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">I4</a></span></li>
</ul>

## Policy and Institutions

<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Policy challenges:</strong> The Supreme Court is reviewing concerns that a High Court order on ethanol allocation could disrupt national policy, alongside debates regarding the potential impact of international judicial precedents on Indian regulatory bodies.</li>
  <li><strong>Political demands:</strong> The BJP has formally requested that the Tamil Nadu government cancel the 3.5% internal quota for Muslims based on caste.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5&amp;hl=en-US&amp;gl=US&amp;ceid=US%3Aen" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">W2</a></span></li>
</ul>
